seo.title: Semaglutide Cost Guide: Wegovy, Ozempic, Coverage and Savings
seo.description: US-first semaglutide cost guide covering official savings programs, insurance friction, self-pay pathways, and what to verify before starting treatment.

trust.disclaimer: Pricing, savings eligibility, and coverage rules can change quickly. Verify all current figures and terms directly on official brand pages before acting.

marketSummary: In the United States, semaglutide costs depend on brand, indication, dose, insurance design, and whether the prescription is filled through an official self-pay or savings pathway.
listPrice: Verify current pricing directly on official Wegovy and Ozempic savings pages before starting, because list pricing, self-pay offers, and eligibility terms can change.
cashPayRange: Official self-pay offers exist for some semaglutide brands, but real monthly cost depends on brand, dose, fulfillment path, and whether you are new to a given savings program.
insuranceSummary: Commercial coverage varies widely. Type 2 diabetes coverage does not automatically mean obesity coverage, and weight-management prescriptions often face stricter formulary and prior-authorization rules.
couponSummary: Official manufacturer savings programs may reduce out-of-pocket cost for eligible commercially insured patients, and some self-pay introductory offers are available through official fulfillment channels.
priorAuthSummary: Prior authorization is common when semaglutide is prescribed for obesity or when plans require documentation of BMI, comorbidities, or prior treatment attempts.

faqs.question: What is the safest way to check current semaglutide pricing?
faqs.answer: Use the official Wegovy and Ozempic pricing or savings pages first, then confirm the exact cash-pay or insured amount with the prescribing service and dispensing pharmacy.

faqs.question: Does semaglutide coverage look the same for obesity and diabetes?
faqs.answer: No. Coverage pathways can differ materially by indication, brand, and benefit design. A plan may cover Ozempic for type 2 diabetes but not cover Wegovy for obesity.

faqs.question: Should I compare only the advertised savings-card number?
faqs.answer: No. You should also check dose-specific eligibility, refill timing rules, channel restrictions, prior-authorization requirements, and whether the offer counts toward deductible or out-of-pocket limits.

cta.notes: Use provider matching when you need help comparing insured and self-pay treatment paths.

sources.title: Check Cost and Insurance Coverage | Wegovy
